behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the two files, lists all rAF-dependent test names, names the persistent dataStore test, mentions the :::invalid selector, encodeURIComponent requirement, and stderr:'pipe' removal.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken (double-await, inconsistent skipping, missing encoding) without specifying exact replacement code or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex patterns with \w+ for helper names (not hardcoded 'itRendering'/'itPersistentDataStore'), accepting any variable name with the correct structural pattern.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests are primarily regex/text inspection of source files via _read_file; test_typescript_syntax_valid runs node --check but most assertions are text-pattern matching, not executing the test behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check for absence of buggy patterns AND presence of correct patterns (e.g., test_chrome_test_async_fix requires both no double-await AND correct single-await pattern).

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 10 expected behaviors from instruction.md (async fix, helper patterns, import, encoding, stderr removal) have corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and descriptive function names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies test files, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and test names in instruction.md match those referenced in tests and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files under investigation which is reasonable given the task domain; does not give exact line numbers or replacement code.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code/test edit task, not a config edit task.
